title: Misato Katsuragi Ex Pilot
wikitext:
[]: Misato Katsuragi: Ex-Pilot?             Quote Post by pen-pen » Sun May 22, 2011 10:01 pm Question: Why Misato Katsuragi was in the 'middle' of the 2nd Impact was never really explained. Her father's profile is a mystery.. & how did he come across an 'escape pod' for Misato? He worked at NERV as well? Theory: She was 'one' of the early test pilots for the Provisional Test Units while Gendo was perfecting the technology. After the 2nd Impact, NERV 'found' her & took her back in while erasing 'part' of her memory as a test pilot(failed experiments). Later she is employed by Nerv HQ's Tactical Operations branch... At the same time, she may have re-occurring 'flash memories' of her past & have this nagging feeling she needs to uncover the 'secrets' or skeletons from Gendo/NERV's closet...

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sun May 22, 2011 10:04 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sun May 22, 2011 10:10 pm In addition to what Seele said above me, there's another obvious issue that comes up in my mind: Why in the world would Nerv want to let her anywhere near them, let alone having her direct their tactical operations, after using her for a nefarious purpose and erasing her memory of it? I mean, that would just be asking for trouble.

[]:             Quote Post by juaxoo » Sun May 22, 2011 10:30 pm how the fuck could she be a ex-pilot, Misato is just the daughter of someone who did notable research for gendo, (and probably almost as crazy as gendo is, for science, of course) they found her, saved her, and treated her mental-illness and psychological stress for 2 years (i think?) during gehrin and when nerv was established they made her a captain all in honor and favor to her dead father. (i remember a brief scene where ritsuko sort-of-like interviews Misato in a cafe, with background narrating Misato's past) obviously she has potential for a leader and commander, and gendo probably just likes female employees that are hot, i saw Seele00TextOnly's thread and all i can say is very good anaylzing and speculations. i agree with some of them but not all

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Mon May 23, 2011 1:02 am Yeah, it's a bit of a stretch to say that they planned to pilot Adam and even more so to say that Misato was intended to pilot Adam. I do stand by my opinion that Misato was the donor whose DNA accidentally triggered Adam's awakening and thus Second Impact, but I don't think that the concept of "piloting" Adam-like entities had even arisen at the time. And even if it had, there's no reason to suppose that Misato would be capable of any sort of synchronization with Adam, a completely alien entity to which she bore no relation whatsoever.

[]:             Quote Post by pen-pen » Mon May 23, 2011 1:19 am I didn't mention anything about piloting Adam. I was referring to one of all those 'rejected' evas like the ones in the EVA graveyards... And when they erased here old memory; they may have implanted new ones- plus re-programed & trained her to become one of their commander/leaders

[]:             Quote Post by juaxoo » Mon May 23, 2011 3:44 am pen-pen wrote: I didn't mention anything about piloting Adam. I was referring to one of all those 'rejected' evas like the ones in the EVA graveyards... And when they erased here old memory; they may have implanted new ones- plus re-programed & trained her to become one of their commander/leaders

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Mon May 23, 2011 7:34 am pen-pen wrote: I didn't mention anything about piloting Adam. I was referring to one of all those 'rejected' evas like the ones in the EVA graveyards... And when they erased here old memory; they may have implanted new ones- plus re-programed & trained her to become one of their commander/leaders

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon May 23, 2011 10:39 am pen-pen wrote: I didn't mention anything about piloting Adam. I was referring to one of all those 'rejected' evas like the ones in the EVA graveyards...

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon May 23, 2011 10:52 am Yui hadn't even invented Evangelions yet. How about Dr. K bringing Misato with him for the same Reason Yui brought Shinji, to show her his work? Maybe that's his attempt to... bring her into his world, in a way. Too bad TI foiled it. He got an escape pod because duh, escape pods are useful. He was probably just lucky not to have been blown away before he could reach it. That's a huuuuuuuge stretch here. An interesting question is whether Misato met Keel or young Gendo there - probably not, and even if she did, she'd probably not recognize the icy authority figures with beard and glasses as theperpetually grinning weirdo she once saw chatting with her father... Now imagine Gendo and Dr. K talking about kids. Gendo: *comes home* Honestly that looser is me in 10 years.... *emo cloud* I'll certainly be a horrible dad... *self-esteem issues**lol irony* Yui: No, I'm sure you won't... *hopeless romantic/optimist* *pats him* Gendo. *looks like he's in dire need of a hug* U sure? Yui: Yeah. *supplies said hug* *and not just a hug* You were away for a lotta time and I'm horny! *wants babies and thus stopped taking the pill without telling Gendo* *and thus, Shinji was concieved*

[]:             Quote Post by pen-pen » Tue May 24, 2011 4:24 am esselfortium wrote: But this doesn't explain what that would actually accomplish. Why not train a leader who you aren't keeping gigantic secrets from involving their own life experiences? Again, this would literally just be asking for things to backfire on them.
